diagrams.title: JSP Request-Response Processing Flow
diagrams.description: A sequential flowchart showing how a client request flows through the JSP container and results in a response
elements: Client Browser (sends HTTP request with .jsp URL)
Web Server (receives request, forwards to JSP Container)
JSP Container (identifies JSP file, performs translation phase)
JSP Compiler (converts JSP to Java Servlet source code)
HTTP Response (HTML sent back to client)
diagrams.purpose: Students understand the complete lifecycle of JSP processing from client request to response generation, including the translation and compilation phases that convert JSP to servlets

diagrams.title: JSP to Servlet Conversion Architecture
diagrams.description: Block diagram showing how a JSP page is transformed into a Java servlet by the container
elements: JSP Actions (become method calls in servlet)
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates that JSP is fundamentally a servlet - students learn how their JSP code translates to Java servlet code, reinforcing the relationship between JSP and Servlets

diagrams.title: JSP Implicit Objects Architecture
diagrams.description: Diagram showing the nine JSP implicit objects and their corresponding servlet equivalents
diagrams.purpose: Students memorize the nine implicit objects available in JSP pages and understand their relationship to servlet API classes, which is essential for writing effective JSP code

diagrams.title: JSP Tag Categories Classification
diagrams.description: Hierarchical diagram showing all categories of JSP tags and their purposes
elements: Directives (<%@ page %>, <%@ include %>, <%@ taglib %>) - page attributes, file inclusion, tag libraries
diagrams.purpose: Provides comprehensive overview of all JSP syntax elements, helping students understand when and how to use each type of tag in their JSP pages

diagrams.title: JSP Lifecycle States and Methods
diagrams.description: State diagram showing the lifecycle states of a JSP page and the callback methods invoked at each stage
elements: _jspService() - handles all client requests, receives HttpServletRequest/Response
diagrams.purpose: Students understand the JSP lifecycle phases and corresponding methods, which is important for resource management and understanding when initialization/destruction code should be placed

charts.title: JSP Scripting Elements Comparison
charts.content: Directives: <%@ %> - Processed at translation time, affects servlet structure, attributes: page, include, taglib | Declarations: <%! %> - Declares instance variables and methods, outside _jspService(), persists across requests | Scriptlets: <% %> - Contains Java code, executes per request, inside _jspService(), for control flow logic | Expressions: <%= %> - Evaluates to string, auto-converted, shorter than scriptlet with out.print()

charts.title: JSP vs Servlets Comparison
charts.content: Servlets: Pure Java code, need to handle HTML via println(), better for business logic, faster after compilation, harder to write/edit HTML | JSP: HTML with embedded Java, easier for designers, better for presentation, compiles to servlet automatically, easier to maintain HTML structure | When to use: Servlets for controller logic, complex processing, APIs | JSP for view/presentation layer, displaying data

charts.title: JSP Request Processing Flowchart
charts.content: Start → Client requests JSP → Container checks if JSP modified? → Yes: Recompile to servlet → No: Use cached servlet → Call jspInit() if first access → Call _jspService(request, response) → Generate HTML → Send response → End. If error: Check for errorPage → Display error page if configured.

charts.title: JSP Directive Attributes by Type
charts.content: Page Directive: language=java, contentType=text/html, import=java.util.*, errorPage=error.jsp, isErrorPage=false, session=true, buffer=8kb, autoFlush=true, extends=HttpJspBase, pageEncoding=UTF-8 | Include Directive: file=header.jsp (static inclusion at translation time) | Taglib Directive: uri=http://java.sun.com/jsp/jstl/core, prefix=c

branches: Purpose: Dynamic web content, View layer in MVC
JSP Container: Translates, Compiles, Executes JSP
JSP Scripting Elements: Directives, Declarations, Scriptlets, Expressions
JSP Implicit Objects: request, response, out, session, application, config, pageContext, page, exception
JSP Actions: include, forward, useBean, param, plugin, setProperty, getProperty
JSP EL (Expression Language): ${}, accessing beans, collections, operators
JSTL: Core tags (c:if, c:forEach, c:choose), Formatting tags, SQL tags
mindMaps.purpose: Comprehensive overview connecting JSP concepts, architecture, and components - helps students see the big picture of JSP in Java web development and its role in enterprise applications
